medical_history.chief_complaint: inexplicable progressive weight gain, muscle tremor, general fatigue, perspiration, hypercholesterolemia, exophthalmos, an enlargement of the thyroid gland, and typical cushingoid features like abdominal striae, 'moon face', central obesity, and a 'buffalo hump'
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Presented in September 2003 at the age of 41 years with symptoms. Laboratory testing showed increased 24 h urinary free cortisol, significant elevation of basal serum cortisol and ACTH, and hyperthyroidism. Ectopic ACTH syndrome was suspected. Subtotal thyroidectomy and neurosurgical removal of a small lesion of the neurohypophysis did not lead to remission of hypercortisolism. Treatment was started with metyrapone combined with hydrocortisone replacement.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Hypercortisolism, diabetes mellitus, hypertension, hypercholesterolemia, multinodular goiter

physical_examination.general_examination: Obesity (110 kg bodyweight, BMI 34.7 kg/m²), pulse of 70 bpm, no signs of cardiopulmonary decompensation but cushingoid features like 'moon face', plethora, 'buffalo hump' and central obesity
